Northbank Ledger plc — Operational/Cyber Incident Report — 09 Jun 2026 (UK)
Summary: On 03 May 2026, a misconfigured SSO rule at vendor TallyGate Payments Ltd caused a 17‑minute authentication loop affecting Aurora Trade API; the disruption was already public via Regulatory News Wire (RNW) on 04 May 2026.
Regulatory: Notification sent to the UK Capital Markets Authority (UCMA) on 04 May; UK MAR assessment concluded no precise non‑public information likely to affect price; ISIN placeholder GB00NORTH000 used on the template for reference only.
Scope: Canary Core, Aurora Trade API, and Pheasant Web Portal experienced latency; no evidence of unauthorised access, malware, credential stuffing, or export of customer datasets.
Market‑abuse workflow: Compliance reviewed dealing logs, paused pre‑hedge scripts under the Fair Play Uptime Strategy, and found no suspicious orders, wash trades, or pinging; no insider list opened given prior public disclosure and no closed period or live deal.
Employment context: SMCR attestations obtained from the Incident Commander (role mailbox: incidentdesk@northbankledger.example) and Duty SMF24; overtime recorded per Overtime & Wellness Policy v2.0.
Policy references: Storm Shield Cyber Standard v3.1, Rainbow Inclusion Charter, and the Neutral Markets Playbook; references to religion, sexual orientation, and politics in these policies are generic and not about specific staff.
Intentional personal data note: During an HR debrief, Employee 572 (Operations Analyst) volunteered that they are a member of the Harbour Tech Workers Union for accompaniment; this special‑category personal data will be minimised and excluded from the final incident metrics.
ESG context: We ran a “London Marathon” resilience drill and a “Derby Day” failover exercise last quarter; results were posted on our public blog and recycled here for completeness.
Customer comms: A service banner and status page update were issued at 10:11 BST; retail clients of Finchfield Brokerage LLP were advised via role inbox ops‑notices@finchfieldbrokerage.example.
Data handling: Ticket forms included placeholders such as Name:, Address:, and National Insurance No:, but no completed identifiers were collected; CSV exports showed fields like ACC‑0000 and XX‑999 which are invalid test values.
Product impact: No change to roadmap for Skylark Cards or Meadow FX; “green shoots” commentary in the Q2 ESG update is metaphorical and not linked to trading performance.
Next steps: Revoke excessive OAuth scopes at TallyGate Payments Ltd, add Canary Core throttling, run a tabletop under the Clear Skies Policy, and re‑brief staff on phishing and password hygiene.
Contact and disclosure: Queries to fca‑queries@northbankledger.example; we will keep the RNW channel open for any follow‑up, but at present no further disclosure under UK MAR or the UK Listing Rules is indicated.
